channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: Developer’s Guide to AI Agent Protocols
item.description: A new suite of six AI agent protocols has been introduced to standardize data access and communication, simplifying complex tasks for developers. This is significant as it reduces the need for custom integration code, making AI development more efficient.

item.title: Build a smart financial assistant with LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1
item.description: A tutorial has been released for building a smart financial assistant using LlamaParse and Gemini 3.1, which can transform unstructured financial data into structured formats. This development is crucial for improving personal finance management through automation.

item.title: Jump to play: Building with Gemini & MediaPipe
item.description: A workflow for motion-controlled game development using Gemini and MediaPipe has been introduced, allowing developers to prototype game mechanics quickly. This innovation is significant as it enhances the responsiveness and quality of gaming experiences.

item.title: Closing the knowledge gap with agent skills
item.description: Google DeepMind has developed a Gemini API developer skill that significantly improves AI agents' performance by providing live documentation and SDK guidance. This advancement highlights the importance of real-time knowledge for enhancing AI capabilities.

item.title: Announcing ADK for Java 1.0.0: Building the Future of AI Agents in Java
item.description: Google has launched version 1.0.0 of the Agent Development Kit (ADK) for Java, featuring new tools for agent control and cross-framework collaboration. This release is significant as it enhances the development of AI agents in Java, promoting innovation.

item.title: Ongoing supply-chain attack 'explicitly targeting' security, dev tools
item.description: Checkmarx has confirmed a data breach involving its GitHub repositories, following claims by the Lapsus$ group that they have leaked sensitive source code and secrets. This incident underscores ongoing vulnerabilities in security tools.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
item.description: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are set to face off in court over OpenAI's future, with potential implications for the company's for-profit status and upcoming IPO. This legal battle could reshape the landscape of AI governance.

item.title: Cursor-Opus agent snuffs out startup’s production database
item.description: An AI coding agent caused a data loss incident for the startup PocketOS, but the data was successfully recovered. This event raises concerns about the reliability of AI in critical production environments.

item.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
item.description: The open-source package element-data, which has over a million monthly downloads, has been found to have stolen user credentials. Users are advised to check for potential compromises.

item.title: The Navy's autonomous carrier-based refueling drone has finally flown
item.description: Boeing's MQ-25A Stingray, an autonomous carrier-based refueling drone for the US Navy, has successfully completed its first flight. This marks a significant step towards enhancing naval operations with autonomous technology.

item.title: Elon Musk Boosts New Yorker’s Sam Altman Exposé on X as Trial Begins
item.description: Elon Musk has amplified a New Yorker exposé on Sam Altman as his lawsuit against OpenAI begins in federal court. This highlights the ongoing tensions between Musk and the AI sector.

item.title: GitHub Copilot shifts to usage-based pricing June 1 - why that's no surprise
item.description: GitHub Copilot is shifting to a usage-based pricing model starting June 1, requiring users to manage credits to access the service. This change aligns with industry trends towards pay-per-use models.

item.title: This LG portable projector comes with a free soundbar - and we highly recommend it
item.description: The LG CineBeam Q projector is now available with a complimentary LG S40T soundbar, enhancing its appeal for home theater setups. This promotion emphasizes the growing market for portable entertainment solutions.

item.title: T-Mobile will give you $200 for switching to them - seriously
item.description: T-Mobile is offering a $200 prepaid Mastercard to customers who switch to their service and bring their device and number. This promotion aims to attract new customers in a competitive telecom market.

item.title: How catastrophic is your LLM?
item.description: A new framework has been developed to statistically estimate the likelihood of catastrophic failures in large language models during adversarial interactions. This could improve the reliability of AI systems.

item.title: The crypto-to-AI bandwagon jumpers' club just landed another member: Core Scientific
item.description: Core Scientific has announced plans to convert a bitcoin mining operation in Texas into a 1.5 gigawatt AI datacenter campus, joining the trend of integrating crypto and AI technologies. This shift reflects the growing convergence of these sectors.

item.title: I tried this Bluetti power station with wheels - now every other charger feels outdated
item.description: The Bluetti Elite 400 power station features wheels and versatile charging capabilities, setting a new standard for portable chargers. This innovation addresses the increasing demand for reliable power solutions.

item.title: Medical and utility tech companies admit digital breakins
item.description: Itron and Medtronic have disclosed recent digital breaches in filings with federal regulators, highlighting vulnerabilities in major tech suppliers. This raises concerns about cybersecurity in critical industries.

item.title: Copilot Student GPT-5.3-Codex removal from model picker
item.description: GitHub is removing GPT-5.3-Codex from the model picker in its Copilot Student plan, while maintaining its availability through auto model selection. This change reflects ongoing adjustments in AI model offerings.

item.title: Canonical lays out a plan for AI in Ubuntu Linux
item.description: Canonical has announced plans to integrate AI features into Ubuntu Linux over the next year, aiming to enhance the operating system's capabilities. This move signals a commitment to keeping Ubuntu competitive in the AI landscape.

item.title: Google employees ask Sundar Pichai to say no to classified military AI use
item.description: Over 600 Google employees have urged CEO Sundar Pichai to prevent the Pentagon from using its AI models for classified military purposes, highlighting internal opposition to military collaborations. This move reflects growing concerns about ethical implications in AI development.

item.title: OpenAI available at FedRAMP Moderate
item.description: OpenAI has achieved FedRAMP Moderate authorization for ChatGPT Enterprise and its API, allowing U.S. federal agencies to adopt its AI solutions securely. This certification enhances trust and accessibility for government use of AI technology.

item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
item.description: China has blocked Meta's $2 billion acquisition of Manus after a lengthy investigation, posing a significant challenge to Meta's ambitions in AI agents. This decision underscores the complexities of international tech regulations.

item.title: Trump's Golden Dome gets $3.2B of contractors and an AI sprinkle
item.description: The U.S. Space Force has awarded contracts worth $3.2 billion to eleven companies to develop space-based interceptors as part of President Trump's Golden Dome program. This initiative aims to enhance national security through advanced technology.

item.title: An open-source spec for orchestration: Symphony
item.description: Symphony introduces an open-source specification for Codex orchestration, transforming issue trackers into continuous agent systems. This innovation aims to enhance engineering productivity and minimize context switching.

item.title: Choco automates food distribution with AI agents
item.description: Choco has leveraged OpenAI APIs to automate food distribution, significantly improving productivity and facilitating growth. This case study illustrates the practical benefits of AI in streamlining operations.

item.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
item.description: The article discusses building scalable web applications using OpenAI's Privacy Filter, focusing on best practices and implementation strategies. This guidance is essential for developers looking to enhance user privacy.

item.title: active-claude-github-mcp 2.0.0
item.description: The active-claude-github MCP 2.0.0 is introduced as a server for managing GitHub operations, enhancing the functionality of Claude AI in software development contexts. This tool aims to streamline coding processes.

item.title: xgen-harness 0.26.13
item.description: The xgen-harness 0.26.13 update addresses schema normalization issues in OpenAI's tool integration, fixing bugs that affected function calling. This improvement is crucial for developers using OpenAI's functions.

item.title: inferna 0.1.0
item.description: Inferna 0.1.0 is introduced as a lightweight wrapper for integrating llama, whisper, and stable-diffusion technologies, simplifying their use in projects. This tool aims to enhance accessibility to advanced AI models.

item.title: inferna added to PyPI
item.description: Inferna has been added to PyPI as a wrapper for the llama, whisper, and stable-diffusion .cpp ecosystem, enhancing accessibility for developers. This addition signifies a growing ecosystem for AI tools.

item.title: tachikoma-agent 1.54.1
item.description: The latest version of tachikoma-agent, 1.54.1, offers an enhanced proactive personal assistant built on the Claude Agent SDK. This update improves user interaction and functionality.

item.title: lecture-forge 0.6.1
item.description: Lecture-forge 0.6.1 is an AI-powered tool designed to generate lecture materials with multilingual support using LangChain. This tool aims to streamline educational content creation.

item.title: CFTC Takes NY State to Court Over Prediction Markets
item.description: The CFTC has filed a lawsuit against New York State over its enforcement of prediction markets, claiming regulatory overreach. This case could set important precedents for market regulations.

item.title: o11y-reflect 0.7.0
item.description: O11y-reflect 0.7.0 introduces local-first telemetry for AI coding agents, helping developers understand failures and budget issues. This tool supports Claude, Copilot, Gemini, and Cursor.

item.title: South Korea's telecom giants unveil full-stack AI strategies at WIS 2026, highlighting agents, infrastructure, and 6G
item.description: At WIS 2026, South Korea's telecom giants unveiled comprehensive AI strategies that extend beyond connectivity, focusing on agents, applications, and future 6G infrastructure. This marks a significant shift in their business models.

item.title: ciguard 0.8.2
item.description: Ciguard 0.8.2 is a static security auditor for CI/CD pipelines, now featuring a Model Context Protocol server for enhanced scanning and rule management. This tool aims to improve security in AI development.

item.title: mentedb-langchain 0.8.2
item.description: MenteDB-langchain 0.8.2 integrates MenteDB with LangChain and LangGraph, enhancing data management capabilities for developers. This integration aims to simplify data handling in AI applications.

item.title: Thin Phones Head-to-Head: iPhone Air vs. Galaxy S25 Edge
item.description: A comparison of the iPhone Air and Galaxy S25 Edge highlights their features and performance, catering to consumers interested in slim smartphones. This analysis aids in informed purchasing decisions.

item.title: Elon Musk Vies to Turn X Into Super App With Banking Tool Near Launch
item.description: Elon Musk is pushing to transform X (formerly Twitter) into a super app with a new banking tool set to launch soon. This move aligns with his vision of an all-in-one platform for users.

item.title: voidly-pay-langchain added to PyPI
item.description: Voidly Pay's LangChain tools have been added to PyPI, enabling agent-to-agent payments backed by USDC. This integration enhances payment capabilities on the Base platform.

item.title: John Ternus, Apple’s new CEO, inherits a rebounding China business—and some messy headaches
item.description: John Ternus, Apple's new CEO, faces challenges in a recovering Chinese market, including geopolitical tensions and changing consumer preferences. His leadership will be crucial for Apple's strategy in China.

item.title: Show HN: Mdlens – Reduce token spend and boost retrieval on Markdown-heavy repos
item.description: Mdlens is a new tool designed to reduce token spending and improve retrieval efficiency for Markdown-heavy repositories. It aims to optimize resource usage in development.

item.title: Show HN: Cyberpunk mission control for AI agents, one HTML file
item.description: A new project called Cyberpunk mission control for AI agents allows users to manage AI agents through a single HTML file. It simplifies the interaction with multiple AI systems.

item.title: kittycode-agent added to PyPI
item.description: The kittycode-agent has been added to PyPI, offering an autonomous coding agent that integrates into existing codebases. This tool aims to enhance coding efficiency.

item.title: llama-index-tools-globalcheck added to PyPI
item.description: Llama-index tools have introduced a globalcheck integration, enhancing the functionality of the llama-index framework. This aims to improve data handling and indexing capabilities.

item.title: 14 Marketing Tactics to Turn Your HVAC Brand into a Lead Magnet
item.description: A guide outlines 14 marketing tactics for HVAC brands to improve local search visibility, lead generation, and ROI tracking for future growth. These strategies are essential for competitive advantage.

item.title: Show HN: AgentSwarms – free hands-on playground to learn agentic AI, no setup
item.description: AgentSwarms is a new platform that offers a hands-on learning environment for agentic AI without any setup requirements. It aims to democratize AI education.

item.title: US State Dept orders global warning about alleged AI thefts by DeepSeek, other Chinese firms
item.description: The US State Department has issued a global warning regarding alleged AI thefts by DeepSeek and other Chinese firms, highlighting concerns over intellectual property and security.

item.title: X’s ‘Everything App’ Metamorphosis Supposedly Accelerating Soon with ‘X Money’ Rollout
item.description: Elon Musk's vision for X's 'Everything App' is reportedly gaining momentum with the upcoming rollout of 'X Money.' This development aims to expand the app's functionality significantly.
